# awscc_bedrockagentcore_harness (Resource)

Definition of AWS::BedrockAgentCore::Harness resource type - a managed agentic loop service that provides a turnkey solution for running stateful, tool-equipped AI agents.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `execution_role_arn` (String) The ARN of the IAM role that the harness assumes when running.
- `harness_name` (String) The name of the harness.
- `model` (Attributes) The model configuration for the harness. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--model))

### Optional

- `allowed_tools` (List of String) The tools that the agent is allowed to use.
- `authorizer_configuration` (Attributes) The inbound authorization configuration for authenticating incoming requests. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--authorizer_configuration))
- `environment` (Attributes) The compute environment configuration for the harness, including underlying runtime information. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--environment))
- `environment_artifact` (Attributes) The environment artifact for the harness, such as a custom container image. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--environment_artifact))
- `environment_variables` (Map of String) Environment variables to set in the harness runtime environment.
- `max_iterations` (Number) The maximum number of iterations the agent loop can execute per invocation.
- `max_tokens` (Number) The maximum number of tokens the agent can generate per iteration.
- `memory` (Attributes) The AgentCore Memory configuration for persisting conversation context. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--memory))
- `skills` (Attributes List) The skills available to the agent. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--skills))
- `system_prompt` (Attributes List) The system prompt that defines the agent's behavior. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--system_prompt))
- `tags` (Attributes List) Tags to apply to the harness resource. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tags))
- `timeout_seconds` (Number) The maximum duration in seconds for the agent loop execution per invocation.
- `tools` (Attributes List) The tools available to the agent. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tools))
- `truncation` (Attributes) The truncation configuration for managing conversation context. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--truncation))

### Read-Only

- `arn` (String) The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the harness.
- `created_at` (String) The timestamp when the harness was created.
- `harness_id` (String) The unique identifier of the harness.
- `id` (String) Uniquely identifies the resource.
- `status` (String) The current status of the harness.
- `updated_at` (String) The timestamp when the harness was last updated.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `identity` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_bedrockagentcore_harness.example
  identity = {
    arn = "arn"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
### Identity Schema

#### Required

- `arn` (String) The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the harness

#### Optional

- `account_id` (String) AWS Account where this resource is managed
- `region` (String) Region where this resource is managed

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_bedrockagentcore_harness.example
  id = "arn"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
$ terraform import awscc_bedrockagentcore_harness.example "arn"
```
